sidebar.title: ECTOFORM
sidebar.upload_tooltip: Upload STL, STEP, 3DM, OBJ, or IGES file for 3D visualization
sidebar.surface_disclaimer: Calculated surface area: Sum of the areas of all triangles in the 3D mesh. Useful for estimating galvanizing or surface treatment costs.
sidebar.export_ecto_desc:
Export a single .ecto file with model, annotations, and photos bundled together.
Only ECTOFORM can open .ecto files.
sidebar.export_ecto_footer: Single file contains: model + annotations + photos. Recipients open it directly in ECTOFORM.
sidebar.export_3d_pdf_desc:
Export interactive 3D PDF for Adobe Acrobat Reader.
Rotate, pan, and zoom the model inside the PDF.
sidebar.export_3d_pdf_footer: Requires Adobe Acrobat Reader for interactive 3D. Ensure VTK provides vtkU3DExporter (usually via 'pip install -U vtk').
sidebar.no_model_loaded: No 3D model loaded. Please load a model first.
sidebar.no_annotations_drawings_msg:
There are no annotations or drawings to include.
Do you want to export the model as .ecto without them?
sidebar.recipients_msg: Recipients can open this file in ECTOFORM.

scale.title: 📐 Drawing Scale
scale.instructions:
1. Upload a drawing (PDF/image)
2. Use scroll wheel to resize the drawing proportionally
3. Align the drawing's reference dimension with the ruler frame
4. Enable Ruler Tool to measure

converter.title: 🔄  Convert File
converter.subtitle: Select a STEP or 3DM file to see available conversions

help.title: ❓ Help & FAQ
help.subtitle: Click a question to see the answer

topics.question: How do I load a 3D file?
topics.answer:
Click the Upload button in the sidebar or drag-and-drop a file onto the viewer area.

Supported formats: STL, STEP, OBJ, IGES, DXF, 3DM.

You can also open multiple files in separate tabs using the '+' button.

topics.question: How do I measure dimensions?
topics.answer:
1. Click the Ruler icon in the toolbar.
2. Click two points on the model to create a measurement.
3. The distance is shown in the current unit (mm by default).

You can change units from the sidebar dropdown.

topics.question: How do I annotate a model?
topics.answer:
1. Click the Annotation mode button in the toolbar.
2. Click on the 3D model to place an annotation marker.
3. Type your note in the annotation panel that appears on the right.

Annotations are saved when you export to .ecto format.

topics.question: How do I use Technical Overview?
topics.answer:
1. Switch to the Technical Overview tab in the mode bar.
2. Upload a document image or PDF.
3. Use the annotation tool to place numbered callout arrows.
4. Fill in metadata (title, manufacturer, dates) in the sidebar.
5. Export as .ecto or PDF.

topics.question: How do I calibrate Drawing Scale?
topics.answer:
1. Switch to the Drawing Scale tab.
2. Upload a technical drawing (PDF, JPG, or PNG).
3. Use the scroll wheel to resize the drawing proportionally.
4. Align the drawing's known dimension with the ruler frame.
5. Once calibrated, enable the Ruler Tool to take accurate measurements.

Use 'Add Reference' to place extra reference markers anywhere on the drawing.

topics.question: How do I apply textures?
topics.answer:
1. Click the Texture mode button in the toolbar.
2. Upload texture images in the texture panel.
3. Drag a texture from the panel and drop it onto a part of the 3D model.

The texture will be applied to the selected surface.

topics.question: How do I take screenshots?
topics.answer:
1. Click the Screenshot mode button in the toolbar.
2. Adjust the view as desired.
3. Use the screenshot panel controls to capture and save the image.

Screenshots can be saved as PNG files.

topics.question: How do I export my work?
topics.answer:
ECTOFORM supports multiple export options:

• Scaled STL — Export with a custom scale factor from the sidebar.
• .ecto — Bundle the model, annotations, and metadata into a single file.
• PDF — Export Technical Overview as a formatted PDF report.
• Scaled Drawing — Export calibrated drawings from the Drawing Scale mode.
• Screenshots — Save viewport captures as PNG images.

topics.question: What file formats are supported?
topics.answer:
Import formats:
  • STL (binary & ASCII)
  • STEP / STP (AP203, AP214)
  • OBJ (with MTL materials)
  • IGES / IGS
  • DXF (3D entities)
  • 3DM (Rhino)
  • .ecto (ECTOFORM bundle)

Export formats:
  • STL (scaled)
  • .ecto (annotated bundle)
  • PDF (technical report)
  • PNG (screenshots & scaled drawings)

topics.question: How do I use multi-part models?
topics.answer:
When a multi-part model is loaded (e.g., STEP or OBJ with groups):

1. Click the Parts mode button in the toolbar.
2. The parts panel lists all detected components.
3. Click a part to select/highlight it in the 3D view.
4. Toggle visibility of individual parts.

license.title: ECTOFORM
license.instructions:
Please enter your license key to activate the application.
You can obtain a license key from the software vendor.
license.activated_msg:
Your license has been activated successfully!
You can now use the application.

passcode.set_hint:
This passcode will be required to edit the .ecto file.
Anyone can still view the file without a passcode.

⚠ Important: This passcode can only be reset from this
machine's application. Keep it safe.
passcode.enter_hint: Enter the passcode to unlock editing.
passcode.recovery_hint: Lost passcode? Ask the file creator to reset it from their machine.
passcode.min_length_error: Passcode must be at least 4 characters.
